title: Clean Fuels Pathways Trend Sheet by Market - Q4 2025
content: LCFS Markets See Rising Pathway Activity and Expanding Fuel Diversity Californiaâ€™s LCFS saw a sharp rise in new pathway registrations in 2024 Q4, with 474 additions, followed by steady growth through 2025 Q1â€“Q3. Oregonâ€™s CFP recorded a major spike in 2025 Q1, registering 218 new pathways afterÂ  subdued quarters. Overall, California continues to lead with the highest number of active pathways. The fuel-type mix is becoming more diverse, with hydrogen, renewable diesel, alternative jet fuel, and RNG expanding their share. In British Columbia, renewable diesel and jet fuel pathways continue to increase. The California Air Resources Board (CARB) has also finalized LCFS amendments effective July 1, 2025, aimed at deeper emission reductions, faster zero-emission infrastructure rollout, and stronger clean-fuel investment.
author: Sanyam Malik
